title: Exposure routes and support phase
summary: Fifty-first structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-4 wave first converts `treeline-low-fell` on `edge-pattern-line` into a stronger ordered exposure chapter, then adds one tiny interpretation support follow-on through an earned `place-tab` support that pins authored place-reading prompts to the existing `TODAY` strip.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the inland exposure Route v2 handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The alpine half now wants the same purposeful outing feel as the forest and coast.

execution_order.goal: Implement the ordered `treeline-low-fell` exposure conversion on `edge-pattern-line`.
execution_order.why_now: That capstone already owns the alpine exposure read, but it still behaves more like a loose checklist than an authored outing.

execution_order.goal: Review the inland Route v2 conversion.
execution_order.why_now: The route needs to feel more game-like without becoming harsh or cluttered.
A tiny interpretation support can make outings feel more chosen rather than merely guided.

execution_order.goal: Implement the earned `place-tab` interpretation support on the existing support row.
execution_order.why_now: The next support gain should help reading the place, not just reaching it.
The support should stay tiny and optional.

guardrails: Keep the support tiny and optional.
Do not add a quest log or inventory shell.
Use existing world-state, support, and filing seams.
Keep the tone cozy and notebook-first.
Keep `treeline-low-fell` inside the current `assemble-evidence` Route v2 seam instead of inventing another route type.
Keep the interpretation support in the existing support row instead of adding a new shop or planner surface.
acceptance_focus: The `treeline-low-fell` alpine capstone now feels more like a deliberate mini-adventure.
One more tiny support helps interpretation rather than just travel.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-exposure-routes-and-support-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-inland-exposure-route-v2-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-place-tab-interpretation-support-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's current wave: `treeline-low-fell` is already converted, so the next step is to add one tiny earned `place-tab` support that keeps interpretation in the existing support row by pinning authored place-reading prompts to the `TODAY` strip on `edge-pattern-line`.
